methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Cellcolabs is a Series A-stage biotech company that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private companies and 3 public peers in the Biotech & Health sector, including later-stage companies such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, and Asimov.

Two quantitative methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $50M based on the $25M raised proxy. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables with valuations ranging from $100M to $3.3B and a median of $805M. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales is not executable absent disclosed revenue data. The triangulated valuation range spans $42M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $428M across the two applied methods.

Key uncertainties include significant dispersion in the peer set—the $805M median reflects later-stage (Series B) companies, which likely command valuations above current Series A positioning. Stage-progression conservatively suggests $50M, while peer-median anchoring may overweight more advanced companies. A reasonable working range is $50M–$200M, contingent on product-stage maturity, clinical progress, and capital efficiency relative to peer cohorts.
